title: hpke.rs: room key AAD is hex(X25519_pk), not recipient PubId — cross-client interop broken
description: In tui/src/event.rs, the sealed_deliver and send_chat paths compute the HPKE AAD for the room key as: pk.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect() — a hex encoding of the raw X25519 public key bytes. unseal_message requires this AAD to match exactly. Any other client (wasm, daemon, FFI) that seals to the room key using a different AAD string (e.g., the actual PubId, or a different encoding) will produce a ciphertext that the TUI cannot open. The convention is documented nowhere. A second TUI client on the same relay session will work (it uses the same formula), but any future client that guesses the AAD should be a standard PubId will silently fail decryption on every sealed broadcast.
